notes: THE PROPHET OF THE RED WORLD · L. Rafayette Hubbard, 1948, unsent · Rocha's coffee-stained copy, marginalia in blue. Runtime excerpts for the Codex MANUSCRIPT tab. Each entry unlocks with the game chapter that adapts it. The endpaper unlocks only with THE CORRECTION.

front_matter.heading: TITLE PAGE
front_matter.typescript:
THE PROPHET OF THE RED WORLD

a novel by L. RAFAYETTE HUBBARD
Pasadena, California · 1948

Manuscript · 78,432 words
For consideration by Street & Smith Publications
front_matter.marginalia: he never sent it.  street & smith never saw it.  he shelved it in september '48.  we found it in a box marked 'personal, do not repub' in the estate sale.  the estate never checked the box.

front_matter.heading: DEDICATION
front_matter.typescript:
For M.,
who saw more of me than most
and stayed anyway.
front_matter.marginalia: M is Marjorie Cameron.  hubbard dedicated this book to her while he was busy with Parsons' actual partner.  which is a specific level of gall.  we kept the dedication in the title card · our sara nai's arc is what we did about it.

chapters.covers: I. The Laboratory · II. The Passage
chapters.typescript: John Whiteside stood before the assembled instruments of his art, and knew, as few men have ever known anything, that he stood at the hinge of the ages.  The lesser minds of the Institute had laughed.  They would not laugh long.  The candle flames stood erect as soldiers as he spoke the first syllable of the Operation, and it may be said without exaggeration that the room itself inclined toward him, as toward a king.
chapters.marginalia: the real parsons' notebooks from this period are full of DOUBT.  crossed-out lines.  'try again.'  hubbard writes him as a man who has never once been unsure.  we gave the game's jack the crossed-out lines back.  see: the text box where you get most of the words right.

chapters.covers: III. The Sand · IV. The Circle
chapters.typescript: The red sands of that elder world received him as a rightful heir.  The four-armed barbarian chieftain, seeing at once the natural authority in the Earthman's bearing, knelt · for command is written on some men in a script every race can read.
chapters.marginalia: we made hel velli NOT kneel.  he watches jack for three days and makes him fight a joke-duel about a briefcase.  authority you have to earn is the only kind our game respects.  also 'barbarian' · hel velli reads three languages.

chapters.covers: V. The Court of the Kyrindi · VI. The First Working
chapters.typescript: The silver princess of the Kyrindi had known many suitors, but none like this stranger from beyond the sky, who spoke of powers her philosophers had only guessed at.  Her eyes were pools a man might drown in gladly, and she attended his every word as a student attends a master.
chapters.marginalia: sara nai is a SCHOLAR.  in our version SHE teaches HIM · the kelait manuscripts, the mourning cycle, what the mines are.  'pools a man might drown in' is doing a lot of work for a man who never once wrote what she was reading.

chapters.covers: VII. The Hunt · VIII. The Mountain
chapters.typescript: Of the small folk of the mines little need be said, save that they were grateful, in their fashion, and that their gratitude took the form of service gladly rendered to the man who had broken their chains with his arts.
chapters.marginalia: 'little need be said.'  ONE SENTENCE.  he freed them in one sentence for the applause and moved on.  mother kanel, yr, the kitchen, the salt · all of chapter 4 is us saying MUCH needs to be said.  this chapter is why i took the QA job seriously.

chapters.covers: IX. The Oracle · X. The Betrayal · XI. The Great Work
chapters.typescript: That a lesser man had sought to claim the Prophet's rightful place among the Order will surprise no student of human nature.  Envy attends greatness as the shadow attends the flame.  But the Earthman saw through the usurper as through window-glass, for no deception can long survive the gaze of an awakened will.
chapters.marginalia: the 'usurper' chapter is hubbard yelling at parsons for noticing.  in the real 1946, the money left with hubbard and so did sara northrup.  we built RONSON out of this chapter · fourteen years on parsa, a religion that finally sells, twelve minutes to pitch you.  the soliloquy is his own letters, rearranged.

chapters.typescript: And so the Great Work was accomplished, and the Prophet ascended to his reward, and the red world knew ever after that it had been visited by the greatest of the men of Earth · whose name, in their reverent tongue, they speak still.
chapters.marginalia: his name, in their reverent tongue.  jack dies in this book so the narrator can inherit the applause.  our six endings are six different answers to this one paragraph.  the true one is the game itself.
